questionText: The diameter, $X$ mm, of pebbles on a beach is modelled by $X \sim N(30, 5^2)$. Find $P(X > 35)$, giving your answer to $4$ significant figures.

steps.description: State the distribution and what is required
steps.explanation: The diameters are normal with mean $\mu = 30$ mm and standard deviation $\sigma = 5$ mm. We want the probability that a pebble is wider than $35$ mm.
We change the diameter into a $z$-score by subtracting the mean and dividing by the standard deviation, so we can use the standard normal distribution.
The diameter $35$ is $5$ mm above the mean, which is exactly one standard deviation. So $35$ mm corresponds to $z = 1$.
We want the area to the right of $z = 1$, which is $1$ minus the left-hand cumulative area $\Phi(1)$.
The cumulative value at $z = 1$ is $0.8413$, so the upper tail is $1 - 0.8413 = 0.1587$ to $4$ significant figures.
So about $15.9\%$ of pebbles are wider than $35$ mm. This matches the well-known fact that roughly $16\%$ of a normal distribution lies more than one standard deviation above the mean.
